result.reasoning: Samples are explicitly annotated as TCR libraries from a single-cell 5' CITE-seq experiment studying COVID-19 infection and vaccination in PBMCs. | Samples correspond to distinct 10x Chromium 5' transcriptomic (GEX) or targeted immune repertoire (TCR) libraries sequenced as part of a multi-modal CITE-seq experiment studying PBMC T-cell responses to COVID-19 infection and vaccination vaccines. | Samples are explicitly separated by omic layer ('GEX' in title, '5' mRNA library' in description) and belong to an overarching CITE-seq experiment studying COVID-19 vaccine/infection responses in PBMCs. | Samples split by layer where GEX targets RNA transcripts via 5' scRNA-seq and ADT targets surface proteins and hashtags via CITE-seq (TotalSeq-C), originating from healthy vaccinated or previously COVID-19 infected donor PBMCs. | The series characterizes responses in PBMC of COVID-19 infected and vaccinated individuals; these specific samples are isolated CITE-seq (ADT + HTO hash) libraries derived from TotalSeq-C compatible chemistry on the 10x Chromium 5' platform. | Experiment is a multiplexed CITE-seq dataset studying COVID-19 infection and vaccination from PBMC samples using 10x Chromium 5' chemistry (TotalSeq-C for ADT and cell hashing, and 5' for GEX). | Samples are part of a CITE-seq experiment capturing 5' GEX, TCR, ADT, and cell hashing (multiplexed groups) in PBMCs from COVID-19 infected/vaccinated donors, split by measurement layer.
